title: Tom Brady reveals that he has ‘explored a lot of different things’ about making an NFL comeback
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Tom Brady, 48, explored NFL rules for a potential return after acquiring a minority stake in the Raiders.
NFL policy requires Brady to divest his Raiders ownership to play, citing no active players or team employees can hold ownership.
Brady remains retired, engaging in pursuits like broadcasting and ownership, despite interest in playing again.

title: Police release bodycam footage of Plymouth butcher knife standoff
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Plymouth police released bodycam footage of a standoff with an armed man holding a woman at knifepoint.
The suspect barricaded himself in a Clark Road home with his estranged wife and four children present.
Officers negotiated for 45 minutes before the suspect surrendered; he faces over a dozen criminal charges.

title: MLB debuts automated strike challenge system for 2026 season. Here’s what you need to know.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: MLB introduces the Automated Ball-Strike Challenge System, allowing players to contest umpire calls in real time.
Teams start with two challenges per game, retaining them if successful; extras are given in extra innings.
The system uses 5G for instant results but won't be available during certain MLB events like the Field of Dreams game.

title: Livestream: Judge to announce verdict in the Kelsey Fitzsimmons trial
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Judge Jeffrey Karp to announce verdict in Kelsey Fitzsimmons trial at 2 p.m. Thursday.
Kelsey Fitzsimmons, ex-North Andover officer, charged with assault for allegedly pointing gun before being shot.
Fitzsimmons claims the weapon was pointed at herself during a suicide attempt, maintaining innocence.

title: OKC’s Shai Gilgeous-Alexander impressed by ‘playoff feel’ in loss to Celtics
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Boston Celtics defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder, finalizing their regular-season series with a 1-1 split.
Shai Gilgeous-Alexander praised the playoff atmosphere at TD Garden, calling the Celtics a premier organization.
The Celtics are now half a game ahead of the Knicks, competing for second place in the Eastern Conference.

title: Trooper arrested in Boston for OUI was allegedly ‘slumped’ at the wheel with High Noon in cupholder
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: State Police Trooper Donovan Preston was arrested in Boston for drunk driving and holding an open container on Sunday.
Preston appeared confused and uncooperative when found by police, who assisted him with gear shifting through his window.
Preston pleaded not guilty to the charges and has been suspended pending internal discipline and further court proceedings.

title: What you need to know about Brazil vs. France at Gillette Stadium
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Brazil and France will play a friendly at Gillette Stadium on Thursday at 4 p.m., airing on ESPN2 and ESPN+.
The match serves as a World Cup preparation for players and a logistical test for organizers, including MBTA train operations.
Brazil and France, combining for seven World Cup titles, rarely meet outside major tournaments, last clashing in a 1998 final.

title: Closing arguments delivered in trial of Kelsey Fitzsimmons. Here’s what happened.
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Kelsey Fitzsimmons, a former North Andover officer, is accused of assault with a dangerous weapon after being shot by a colleague.
Defense argues Fitzsimmons was not a threat, citing her actions intending suicide and challenging Officer Noonan's quick response as a mistake.
Prosecution asserts Fitzsimmons lied about gun handling, supporting Noonan's decision to fire based on her actions posing a potential threat.

title: 17-year-old killed in Brockton shooting, second fatal shooting in the city this week
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: 17-year-old Jeffry Damestoire was killed in Brockton shooting; accused is 18-year-old Tyree St. Cloud.
Shooting happened after a physical altercation; St. Cloud allegedly fired a Glock 30 handgun, per authorities.
Second fatal shooting in Brockton this week; event at Massasoit Community College postponed due to community impact.

title: Back Bay restaurant named among The Hollywood Reporter’s most anticipated global openings for 2026
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Rosa y Marigold, a Back Bay restaurant, was named a top anticipated opening in Hollywood Reporter's 2026 Travel Issue.
Owners JuanMa Calderón and Maria Rondeau are known for acclaimed eateries Celeste in Somerville and La Royal in Cambridge.
The 100-seat restaurant will feature Peruvian classics and chifa-inspired dishes, set to open at 400 Newbury St. this spring.
